preview_text:
The model rates Turkey as a slight favorite, assigning them a 50% win probability against Paraguay's 20%. That 30-percentage-point gap suggests the model views Turkey as the more likely victor, though the underdog retains a plausible path to victory.

The expected outcome distribution reveals a competitive match. A draw registers at 30%, indicating the teams are close enough in strength that neither side's advantage is commanding. The spread across all three outcomes is relatively even, with no single result dominating; this points to genuine uncertainty rather than a heavily tilted affair.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps of 3 percentage points or less. Polymarket pricing tracks the books similarly. The modest alignment suggests the available information is producing consistent probability estimates; the dashboard does not claim to identify which source is most accurate.
